assertion.text: Erdős Problem #714: declared status 'open'. Formalized: no. Is it true that\[\mathrm{ex}(n; K_{r,r}) \gg n^{2-1/r}?\] Current best: K\"{o}v\'{a}ri, S\'{o}s, and Tur\'{a}n \cite{KST54} proved\[\mathrm{ex}(n; K_{r,r}) \ll n^{2-1/r}\]for all $r\geq 2$. Brown \cite{Br66} and, independently, Erd\H{o}s, R\'{e}nyi, and S\'{o}s \cite{ERS66}, proved the conjectured lower bound when $r=3$. When $r=2$ it is known that\[\mathrm{ex}(n;K_{2,2})=\left(\frac{1}{2}+o(1)\right)n^{3/2}\](see [768], since $K_{2,2}=C_4$). Prize: no. Tags: graph theory, turan number.
